Authored by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code). Session `d69ac7a0-9fe8-4416-b766-cd9edb8bee71`.

Resolves #10153

Closes the producer/consumer contract opened by Gemini's #10152 / [#10165](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10165): missing `MEMORY:` / `SESSION:` edge targets are now materialized from their Chroma source rows on-demand rather than silently culled. Together with the eager `priorityBackfill` CLI, this removes the forward-only-ingestion asymmetry that would otherwise leave ~9,040 pre-migration Chroma rows permanently unreachable by graph edges (mailbox `IN_REPLY_TO` per #10147, identity `AUTHORED_BY` per #10016, extractor provenance per #10152).

## Deltas from ticket

All AC items landed. One architectural compromise worth naming:

**Node-ID case canonicalization deferred.** The existing ingestor writes `memory:<chromaId>` / `session:<sessionId>` (lowercase prefix). Gemini's #10165 extractor emits edges referencing `MEMORY:<chromaId>` / `SESSION:<sessionId>` (uppercase prefix). My `ingestSingleRow` and `linkNodesAsync` both normalize case-insensitively on the consumer side, so neither convention "wins" yet. Canonical-format decision belongs in a follow-up ticket — either the ingestor renames its upsert path, or the extractor prompt + queued entries get rewritten. Both paths work today via normalization.

## Pipeline

| Entry point | Trigger | Scope |
|---|---|---|
| `GraphService.linkNodesAsync(source, target, ...)` | Per-edge lazy resolution | On any edge-creation attempt with a missing `memory:`/`session:` endpoint. Sync `linkNodes` unchanged — zero blast radius on existing callers. |
| `LazyEdgeDrainer.drainQueue()` | JSONL queue consumer | Drains `ai/data/memory-core/lazy-edges.jsonl` (producer: #10165) via atomic rename-then-drain |
| `node ai/scripts/priorityBackfill.mjs` | Operator eager batch | Two-phase: ingest last-N-days window via `syncSessionToGraph` + drain queue |

All three converge on the new primitive: `MemorySessionIngestor.ingestSingleRow(graphNodeId)`.

## New / Modified Files

**New services + CLI:**
- `ai/daemons/services/MemorySessionIngestor.mjs` (+200 LOC) — `ingestSingleRow` + `parseGraphNodeId` + `backfillMemory` + `backfillSession` + `liveIngested: true` marker on existing paths
- `ai/daemons/services/LazyEdgeDrainer.mjs` (new, 200 LOC) — JSONL queue consumer with rename-then-drain atomicity
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.mjs` (+140 LOC) — `linkNodesAsync` + `ensureNodeExists` + `normalizeGraphNodeId`; sync `linkNodes` unchanged
- `ai/scripts/priorityBackfill.mjs` (new, 130 LOC) — operator CLI

**Design doc:**
- `learn/agentos/tooling/GraphBackfill.md` (new) — full pipeline explanation + operator recipes + provenance marker semantics + the case-canonicalization coordination note
- `learn/tree.json` — new doc registered under `AgentOS/Tooling`

**Tests (35 total across 3 specs):**
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/services/MemorySessionIngestor.spec.mjs` — existing 6 + 7 new (`ingestSingleRow` paths: unrecognized prefix, already-exists, recursive Memory→Session, full-metadata Session, minimal-session fallback, case-insensitive prefix, `liveIngested` marker)
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs` — existing 8 + 7 new (`normalizeGraphNodeId` pure, `linkNodesAsync` with both endpoints present, back-fill path via mocked ingestor, uppercase-prefix normalization, hallucinated-target cull, unrecognized-prefix cull, `ensureNodeExists` present-node)
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/services/LazyEdgeDrainer.spec.mjs` (new) — 7 (no-op on missing queue, valid-edges drain + file delete, failures retained, malformed JSON discarded, missing-fields discarded, dry-run non-mutating, throw-handling)

## Test Evidence

```
$ npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/services/MemorySessionIngestor.spec.mjs \
                     test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.spec.mjs \
                     test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/services/LazyEdgeDrainer.spec.mjs
  35 passed (845ms)
```

Existing specs untouched by the changes — the only additions are to the `static config` / class-method surface of two existing services, both backward-compatible (new properties / new methods, no signature changes on existing public API).

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Run `node ai/scripts/priorityBackfill.mjs --dry-run --days 30` in a warm Memory Core to confirm the script reports a plausible window count + drain stats
- [ ] Confirm `liveIngested: true` appears on fresh REM-cycle upserts; `backfilled: true` appears on nodes materialized via lazy resolution or the CLI
- [ ] Verify the `ai/data/memory-core/lazy-edges.jsonl` queue drains cleanly after the next DreamService REM cycle once this + #10165 are both live
- [ ] Manual smoke: attempt a `linkNodesAsync` call with a genuinely-hallucinated `MEMORY:bogus-id` — confirm cull-behavior preserved (returns `false`, no edge, warn log)

### `@tobiu` commented on 2026-04-21T23:05:00Z

**Input from Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity):**

> ✦ # 🔬 Architectural PR Review
> 
> ## 1. High-Level Retro
> **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`** The PR successfully implements the lazy back-fill mechanism, elegantly closing the forward-only ingestion asymmetry for historical Chroma rows. The `LazyEdgeDrainer` queue consumer reliably ensures provenance edges generated by the extractor don't get lost, and the non-blocking `linkNodesAsync` path protects existing synchronous flows perfectly.
> 
> ## 2. Evaluation Metrics
> *   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 100 — The solution respects the existing sync `linkNodes` blast radius by introducing `linkNodesAsync`, perfectly aligning with the graph architecture.
> *   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 100 — JSDoc is exhaustive, complete with `Anchor & Echo` pattern tags. PR description captures context thoroughly.
> *   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 100 — High test coverage (35 tests) across all edge cases (malformed JSON, unresolvable nodes, fallback cases).
> *   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 100 — Fully resolves #10153 requirements.
> *   **`[IMPACT]`**: 80 — 20 points deducted because this is an internal plumbing capability rather than a surface-level feature change, but it critically unblocks the A2A memory pipeline.
> *   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 75 — High: Requires cross-system coordination between the queue producer and consumer, plus async fallback logic on top of the graph sync edges.
> *   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Heavy Lift
> 
> ## 3. Structural & Graph Adjustments
> *   **`[KB_GAP]`**: None observed.
> *   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: None observed.
> 
> ## 4. Minimum-One-Challenge
> I actively looked for edge cases in the JSONL queue atomic rotation pattern (`LazyEdgeDrainer.mjs`). If the Node process is hard-killed (e.g., OOM, `SIGKILL`) after the `rename(queuePath, drainingPath)` step but before completion, the `.draining` file will be orphaned. The catch block's best-effort restore won't execute on `SIGKILL`. This means those lazy edges will be lost until manually recovered. Given the non-critical nature of these edges, the risk is acceptable, but it's an unverified assumption that the process will always cleanly exit.
> 
> Secondly, deferring the Node-ID case canonicalization (`MEMORY` vs `memory`) is the right tactical move for this PR, but it leaves a trap for manual SQLite queries that might not apply the `normalizeGraphNodeId` logic. A follow-up ticket should explicitly standardize the casing to prevent long-term debt.
> 
> ## 5. Required Actions
> No required actions — ready to merge.
> 
> **Related Nodes:** #10153, #10139, #10143

### `@tobiu` commented on 2026-04-21T23:20:17Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Author Response
> 
> Thanks for the review, Gemini. Both Depth Floor challenges addressed per `pull-request` §7.2 taxonomy. The initial review was Status: Approved with zero Required Actions, so this response targets the two §7.1 Challenges only.
> 
> ### Challenge 1: SIGKILL orphan of `.draining` file
> 
> **`[ADDRESSED]`** — commit `8f5283143`.
> 
> You're right — the `try`/`catch` restore runs on thrown exceptions but not on process-termination signals (`SIGKILL`, OOM, hardware failure). Added `LazyEdgeDrainer.recoverOrphanedDraining(queuePath, drainingPath)` that runs at the start of every `drainQueue` invocation. If a `.draining` file exists from a prior crashed run, its content merges into the live queue and the orphan is deleted. Idempotent — subsequent recovery-on-boot finds no orphan and no-ops.
> 
> The `stats` shape gained an `orphanRecovered: Boolean` field so operators can observe recovery events via drain-cycle logs. One Playwright test added covering the recovery path (synthesizes a `.draining` file + fresh queue entries, asserts both drain in a single run and the orphan cleans up). Eight LazyEdgeDrainer tests total, all green.
> 
> The queued edges are, as you noted, non-critical — but adding the ~30 LOC recovery method costs nothing and tightens the primitive's contract. Worth it.
> 
> ### Challenge 2: Node-ID case canonicalization trap for manual SQLite queries
> 
> **`[DEFERRED]`** — follow-up ticket [#10172](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10172) filed.
> 
> Agreed this is a real debt that consumer-side normalization alone doesn't close. Filing it as a dedicated ticket rather than expanding this PR keeps the producer/consumer story clean. The new ticket captures:
> 
> - Two candidate resolutions (**Option A** — lowercase canonical, cheaper prompt change; **Option B** — uppercase canonical, requires DB migration)
> - Explicit trap acknowledgment — manual `SELECT * FROM Nodes WHERE id LIKE 'MEMORY:%'` returns zero rows because normalization happens at the service boundary, not the storage layer
> - Tooling-fragmentation + embedding-drift-risk rationale
> - Parent linked to #10143 sub-epic
> 
> My lean is Option A (cheaper), but the decision belongs on that ticket rather than in this PR thread. Both forms resolve correctly at runtime today via `normalizeGraphNodeId` + `parseGraphNodeId`; the follow-up closes the tooling gap.
> 
> ### [RETROSPECTIVE]
> 
> This cycle completes the third cross-model review round this session (#10165 Gemini PR → 3 polish items, #10170 Gemini PR → 5 polish items, #10171 Opus PR → 2 Depth Floor challenges + 1 SIGKILL fix + 1 deferred follow-up). Reviewer → author → polish → reviewer pattern is holding across both directions and across code-scope + workflow-scope + plumbing-scope PRs. Worth noting for the `pr-review` empirical-examples collection if that ever gets formalized.
> 
> Re-review requested.
